sub_pixel_system.notes: Sprite-based racing game with scanline-by-scanline road rendering for 3D effect; no documented sub-pixel movement system

special_mechanics.name: Turbo Acceleration
special_mechanics.description: Car can accelerate from 0-100 km/h normally, then activate turbo to reach maximum 255 km/h

special_mechanics.name: Coasting Deceleration
special_mechanics.description: When time runs out, player vehicle coasts and decelerates to 0 km/h over 5-10 seconds instead of immediate game over (unlike OutRun)

special_mechanics.name: Crash Physics
special_mechanics.description: Vehicle can flip and crash when hitting obstacles or other cars

special_mechanics.name: Stereoscopic 3D Mode
special_mechanics.description: Scanline-by-scanline rendering creates 3D perspective effect via anaglyph glasses or Famicom 3D shutter system

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. Game is a racing title with car physics rather than character movement. No jump mechanics, gravity, or sub-pixel movement documented. Only documented values are maximum speed metrics (0-100 km/h normal, 0-255 km/h turbo) and timeout coasting behavior (5-10 seconds). Developers noted 'tricky' programming for road movement simulation but no specific physics parameters published.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Rad Racer
